reviews.review_text: Excellent game and still very enjoyable in 2026, despite the newer versions!

it is very good better than all civ games i've played I don't know but the graphics the sounds the way the game works it just all fits together so well it is one of my favorite games of all time and for 10 years i have refused to play any other civ game
This is the best version of Civ. 4 was great for its time, but this is a massive improvement. 6 isn't as good. I didn't even bother buying 7.
Why Do you need to share my personal info with governmental agencies to play this game? That's pretty lame. It's just a video game.

An amazing and simple strategy game. It’s not hard to understand, and for players who never really played strategy games before this game is perfect. And don’t let the simplicity fool you, that’s actually what makes the game so great. Personally I spent many hours playing it just to try every civilization, and it was always fun for me. Every game was different.. different maps, different scenarios and something new always happened. The game also has several gamemodes where you can change many settings and it has really nice support for community mods too. I never really had any problems with the game, and I would definitely recommend it to anyone who likes strategy genre. In my opinion, Civilization V is much better than the newer CIVs that came after it.

Honestly it’s the perfect game for evening gaming with friends.

8.3/10
It is entertaining actually, but you have to sink a ton of effort into it to have fun. Honestly, if I put that energy into real life, you'd probably have a much better time.

Almost as old as me but more appealing stylistically than the two new ones. Charming and fun to sink some hours in. Except when you play all day and your pos I mean pc crashes and you lose about six hours of gameplay

16 years after release date, and with 354 hours played, I keep coming back to this game every year.

Just beat the game on Immortal for the first time (Poland, small map, diplomatic victory). Pretty intense for a turn-based strategy game.

Warning: this makes me pull 24 hour sessions, just to finish a game in one stretch.. I eat, I stay hydrated, but please.. just.. one.. more.. turn..
Great game design.  Simple to learn and easy enough to master
Easy game to understand, easy scaffolding to continue learning, diverse strategies to win.  Great overall.

But it's probably the best start for new players to the game. And considering the last two games suck balls. You should give it a chance.
It is a genuinely fun game. Cultural institutions you can invest in, new design, hex grid, combat rework (no more doomstacks), navy is fun now, scenarious are also interesting to play with.

Already play it for a long time so I decide to comeback. There a lot of improvement especially the AI and 2k launcher was remove was a win. 

Despite the game was still fun and challenging but most of the time the AI are just cheating. Some of my playthrough, AI straight up found a religion at starting of Ancient Age. Bankcrupt also not apply to them since they have infinite money, their technology was also climbing too fast. I just play it on normal difficulty. Like 3-4 of AI civilisation reach Medieval era like on 10 turn which is absurd. 

I still love the game and devs keep fck up new Civ so I had to play this anyway and they just make an AI absurbly powerful just to messed you up is stupid.

I grew up playing this game with my dad, I think it'll always be one of my favorites. While there is an incredible amount of information to take in, I still think this is the best game from the Civ series.
I've played a pirated version as a kid/when it first came out. Love it brings back so many memories. Love the art style/direction

There is a good reason why Civ V is seen by some to be peak Civ series. It started out with a fair share of problems but by the end it was refined and balanced to a point where no more could be done to make it better. Other games (including Civ games) have come and gone and they are not seen as equal to Civ V in the way it all gels together.

There is no point harping on about this, there are plenty of reviews that go into the details. This is an easy 10/10.

as a civ II fan (which is the best civ in my opinion), I love this game so much! way better than IV in terms of UX and UI, better than VI and VII overall, it will be hard to surpass the quality of this game

Ignore the 50 hour playtime. I've actually played this game for hundreds of hours already. I've revisited and reinstalled it every couple of years, simply because It's way too addicting. It's addicting to the point where I'm already getting tired of it. The graphics is a bit outdated, and the map can be unstable at times if you play it at higher resolutions. However, the gameplay is all there. It's fun and it's dopamine inducing. 
To be clear, this isn't a managerial game like Sims City. You don't manage resources or build houses. It's all about turn economy. The game is a race between you and other AI players, to see who can create the best damn civilization within a finite number of turns. From the stone ages to the modern era, you must found cities, advance your technologies, strengthen your military, work on your diplomacy, and improve your culture to win over the enemy. This is why early game is so fun. It's the part where you must compete for resources and bountiful locations across the map. And if your early game is good, you'll be one step ahead of winning.

This game is I think without a doubt a much worse game compared to civ 6 if you ever try to look for fun ways to beat AI on harder difficulty. Cultural victory is tied to science progression and getting a lot of wonders with great works slot means you will be very unlikey to get cultural victory on emperor or above. The Ai just conquers everything willy nilly and allows to snowball hard when you are far away and fail to deal with them conquering neighbors when you spawn far away from them in island maps. Sometimes the Ai can be a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ in diplomacy and vote to embargo one of your only luxury resources to deny your cities yield. The mechanics in civ 5 are just not enough to leverage the massive bonus for AI unlike civ 6,and if you neighbor an aggressive AI,just reroll.

"In the first war, Persia survived Rome. In the second, it defended itself. And after the third, Rome was no more." 
     I've done 3 playthroughs so far. Went to space in one, conquered the world in the other, lost in my most recent one. Really tuff cool game.
I really have enjoyed all of the civilization series games and Civilization V is no different.
The game is simple to learn but offers endless ways to have fun.

Played this when it was initially out a bit, and enjoyed it. Recently got back into it. Yes there are a lot of improvements with Civilization VI, but this game is a perfectly fine game to play without unnecessary DLC, and a lot of community support. My only complaints would be about some of the UI issues, and that I do not like the city-state system very much. Yes, you can snowball victories to the point that you get bored of playing before long victories, yes the civilizations can have odd perks, and there's definitely unbalanced civs, but the game is still fun, and it's definitely something good to play when you have some good time.
This is simply the best game out there. This is is a man's game? you wanna be a a big boy with the realy mens? you should play this game.
What can be said that hasn't already. It's the best Civ game to date. Why aren't you playing yet?

Ok where to start 
Positive things:
- Once you get the ropes you are drawn to this game it is a decent time waster. You will think you played 2h and then you see 6h passed by you. *have an alarm clock at the ready seriously.
- Its easy to get in easier to grasp how to play than even other CiV games. 
- It has mod support that adds a ton of community made content that just adds more and more hours for you to experiment and play. 
- Its audiobook podcast friendly game. 
- It has low system spec requirements so even your ancient pc from 2010 that struggles to play modern games will have a decent chance at running this game. 

Negatives: 
- Minor regarding massive micromanaging on large maps but if you fall into my category by playing large maps you asked for it. 
- AI not so smart as you play the game it becomes predictable the only issue then is out of whack vanilla balancing that is relying on nerfing your resources and boosting ai resources lame way to increase difficulty instead of giving the Ai more instructions to think against you. So Ai its well mid. 

The full bundle is just overpriced wait for a huge dam discount before buying. 

All in all I do recommend CiV 5 even over modern CIV games this one still holds good. 
See ya.
Pretty damn good game. At first, if this genre isn't your favourite, it can be quite daunting, but when you play more you begin to understand and then it's fun!

I remember finally getting this game after my pals would glaze it to hell and back (rightfully so!). Being in Highschool and coming home and playing it to no end, even getting my pops into it to the point where I'd be watching him make his turns like a proud father watching his child take their first steps. I highly recommend this game if you are either looking for an intense session where the peacemakers of history are slinging nukes left and right, they got it. If you wanna have a quiet and peaceful game with the most violent leaders in history, they got it. I will forever cherish this game <3 (also could 100% sit in the main menu and bask in the music for hours).

Still the best overall Civ game 16 years later; 'nuff said. Play as Sweden if you want to spread Drain to the rest of the uncivilized world.

still has some thing to fix, for example : too easy once you gained some advantages, so very little challanges in late game

One of the best Civilization games, hands down. Many people's favorite, in fact. While I prefer Civ4 and Civ6. just a wee bit more - this is a solid game in the franchise.
If you want a 4X game, but want a slightly simpler version, this one has pretty intuitive rules and less complication. Really nice for an easy to moderate effort game to relax with.
Game keeps crashing with no explanation to why. When your game crashes while in multiplayer mode, the AI plays for you and ruins any plans you were building towards.

I have now played this game for over 500 hours. I still consider myself a beginner.

However, despite that view...it really is wild to realize that a video game has taught me enough - about navigating resource issues, land disputes, religious differences, mercurial leader negotiations, and international politics and conflicts - to realize that the people currently running my country are some of the dumbest ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ to ever stumble into the halls of power.

Play Civilization V. Educate yourself. We're going to need all the help we can get.

(Also, the game sometimes glitches out on higher-spec systems; switch to DirectX9 to reduce these issues.)

This game sucks ♥♥♥♥. Literally defending an ai state for 40mins only for them to turn around and ally with the enemy?!? WHAT THE ♥♥♥♥? The combat in this game is absolutely horrible, its whoever gets the cheesy meta stat first and just steam rolls the map. My units that WERE SUPPOSED TO COUNTER the enemy units were doing 5 dmg to him. Like what?!?!

This is likely the best version of Civ that I've played, and I have played essentially every version of Civ, all the way back to Civ 1 on the Super Nintendo.
Fun to play. Lots of Stratergies to implement

My dear Civilization:



This game gave me so many beautiful memories. I started playing it when I was 16 with a close friend and then I got my father to play it too.
Each of us has a favorite leader: Casimiro for my friend, Dido for my dad, and Pacal for me.

Every Friday, after waking up early all week, I stayed awake playing with them until I fell asleep at 3 or 4 am, playing just "one" more turn. It was so fun, and I will treasure those moments forever.



This game evokes feelings of betrayal as an ally declares war on you, happiness when your empire is thriving and your plans come to fruition, sadness when the opposite happens, and your last doomed city floods with bombs.

The art direction fits perfectly with the game, making it the best looking civ, music is outstanding, and the game feels good and intuitive to play, even the first times.

Start from a single settler, study technologies, choose politics, build wonders, and work your terrain as you develop into a full-fledged civilization. 


You can focus on military and build the biggest army ever, or fully go into science and get an advantage over the other players, providing you an edge in basically everything, try to become the ally of every city-state and control the world congress, or develop your culture and get a win by tourism.

I recommend playing with the mod CBP (community balance patch), it enhances the experience.



To me, it is one of my favorites, the best 4X game ever created, and a must-play for every turn-based strategy game fan.

“Will you build a civilization that stands the test of time?”
There is not a lot of woke BS... Like Civ7 putting Harriet Tubman as a leader when she couldn't even read or write, then pretending like everything about her isn't a lie.

I will leave a review now, so that my opinion can stand the test of time. There is a reason that Civ V has so many holdouts. Will it win any graphical awards? I would argue it holds up against Civ VI, a matter of aesthetic, but sure, Civ VII has it beat. Nevertheless...

Civ V is to me the ultimate realization of Firaxis' vision for this franchise. Afterwards, they tried to hard to be competitive, or accessible, or historical-adjacent into the realm of fantasy, or distracting comedy. It is not that I do not enjoy fantasy in its own right as a genre, but it was never why I continued to press "one more turn" in Civ. 

Civ V scratched that itch for me, and did so for a long time. It feels like yesterday and forever ago that I first booted this game up off of a disk, and was absolutely blown away by the strategy genre itself. For me, it did not end there, rather it continued to be a fun playing experience as modders brought it to a height that leaves this game replayable to this day.

The Civs, the way time progresses and the game plays out; the way the combat works, it is not overly complicated, nor does it need to be. It manages, to me, to create a satisfying world every time you generate a new hex-grid, every time you find a new bordering civ to call a cur and rival. Those things that suffer the most in retrospect - certain victory types, fickle and inconsistent diplomacy, or truly skin-deep aspects like the city states? Modders took things into their own hands, and so they were all brought to a standard that makes this a worthy play to this day. 

I have never played Civ VII, the very premise is anathema to why I enjoy Civ V, and to a lesser extent VI. You bring the story with these games, your decisions in retrospect become the communal history of a people, however abstracted they may be in comparison to titles from a Paradox, for example. It was always just enough to believe you were that abstraction, and choosing to completely sever that connection is a decision that I hope they seek to correct. Other Civ games, I am thinking of IV and VI specifically, did include some things that I believe could have been beneficial to a V. But as an amalgam of everything this series has accomplished, there is no better medley, to me, than V.

I mean there isn't much I can say about this game that isn't put to shame by me saying I have put in 1000 hours. It is my favorite Civ game. Admittedly, I started with Civ 5 and I know there is a contingent out there that says Civ 4 is. I can only speak to the games I have played. The animation in this game makes more sense to me than Civ 6 or 7 in my opinion, I know it isn't the only reason to play a game but if I am playing a full game of 4-8 hours I need to like the graphics. Honestly my biggest reason I love this game is that; It is just a vibe to me and when I am hanging out at home I usually put a tv series on play civ 5.
Both was and perhaps will be, the last good civilization to be released

Better than Civ 7, 6, 4, and 3. Has the core mechanics of 4 beyond the sword, without going into the cartoony-linearism that 6 has. This is the one that will stand the test of time. Definitely buy on sale.
